channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Dublin protesters demand justice over death of Congolese man
item.description: Dublin protesters demand justice over death of Congolese man

item.title: Rio’s Forever War
item.description: In 2025, Brazil’s deadliest police raid killed 120 people and exposed a pattern of mishandled evidence and impunity.

item.title: Israeli forces cross Lebanon’s Litani River and seize 12th century castle
item.description: Invading Israeli forces have crossed north of Lebanon's Litani River and seized the medieval Beaufort Castle.

item.title: From AFCON to World Cup 2026: How Morocco became a football powerhouse
item.description: Morocco's success is built on an effective strategy, but there has been some upheaval in the run-up to FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Labour triumphs in Malta election, preliminary results show
item.description: Labour Party secures historic fourth term in Malta amid geopolitical and economic instability concerns.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks hit multiple Russian targets, including refinery
item.description: Saratov refinery hit as Ukrainian strikes intensify across Russian regions, damaging civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Trump tightens terms on Iran war deal, US media say
item.description: US officials indicate Tehran may take days to respond to Trump's tougher terms on a potential peace agreement.

item.title: Israel issues more displacement orders in Lebanon, seizes strategic castle
item.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz describes the capture of Beaufort Castle as a significant tactical victory.

item.title: The strait may reopen, but global confidence may not return
item.description: The next phase of the Strait of Hormuz crisis may be defined less by its closure than by conditional access.

item.title: Iran war day 93: Trump won’t ‘rush’ deal; Israel expands Lebanon invasion
item.description: Donald Trump says talks with Iran are progressing 'slowly but surely', as Israel intensifies operations in Lebanon.

item.title: Holder Gauff beaten by Potapova; Sabalenka solid at French Open
item.description: Gauff's exit leaves four-time champion Swiatek and world number one Sabalenka as top contenders for the women's title.

item.title: Missing Syrian chess champion’s children likely dead, authorities say
item.description: Syrian commission confirms deaths of Rania al-Abbasi's six children, missing since 2013 under Bashar al-Assad's rule.

item.title: Panic on beach in northern Israel during Hezbollah strikes
item.description: Videos show people on a beach in Nahariya, Israel running for shelter as Hezbollah rockets are launched.

item.title: France arrests hundreds of rioters nationwide as PSG win Champions League
item.description: French Ministry of Interior says 416 people detained nationwide, including 283 in Paris, after PSG's win over Arsenal.

item.title: Mexico beat Australia 1-0 before World Cup squad submission deadline
item.description: The warm-up friendly simulated World Cup conditions, including cooling breaks for the players, at the Rose Bowl stadium.

item.title: Wembanyama’s Spurs dethrone Thunder to reach NBA Finals against Knicks
item.description: Wembanyama scores 22 and grabs 7 rebounds as Spurs beat OKC 111-103 to book their first NBA Finals appearance since 2014.

item.title: How Nigeria’s ‘algorithmic apothecary’ fuels a surge in risky herbal cures
item.description: Unverified herbal remedies promoted on social media are driving rising health risks and delayed treatment in Nigeria.

item.title: The world hit a 44-year high in executions
item.description: Governments around the world executed more people in 2025 than at any point since 1981.

item.title: Thousands in Peru protest Fujimori presidential run
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators took to the streets of Lima to protest the presidential bid of Keiko Fujimori.

item.title: Emotional scenes as more survivors pulled from flooded Laos cave
item.description: Divers in Laos have rescued four more villagers trapped in a flooded cave. The first survivor was pulled out on Friday.

item.title: Rescues in eastern Syria after the Euphrates River bursts its banks
item.description: Rescue workers have pulled stranded farmers from flood waters in eastern Syria after the Euphrates burst its banks.

item.title: Iran war live: Israel to expand Lebanon invasion; US-Tehran deal in limbo
item.description: Lebanese PM describes Israel's attacks as collective punishment as US warns Iran of strikes if no deal is reached.

item.title: Congress advances US-Israeli military integration plan
item.description: A provision in the 2027 draft US defence bill could bind the two countries' weapons industries closer than ever.

item.title: Al Jazeera exclusive interview with rebel FARC faction in Colombia
item.description: In Colombia, FARC dissidents say they returned to war after a historic peace deal failed to deliver security

item.title: Lebanese army ‘overly stretched’ to fight off latest Israeli invasion
item.description: Geopolitical analyst Joe Macaron says the Lebanese army is ‘overly stretched’ as Israeli troops expand their occupation.
